title: A Paralyzed Man Is Playing Chess By Thinking — Thanks to a Brain Chip
script: In January 2024, Noland Arbaugh became the first human to receive Neuralink's brain chip. He is paralyzed from the shoulders down. 8 weeks later, he was playing chess, browsing the web, and gaming — all by thinking. The N1 chip reads his brain signals, and a computer translates them to cursor movements. Zero physical movement required. Brain-computer interfaces just crossed from science fiction into everyday reality.
